# gs run precommit-checks --fix hands failing check output to Claude
# for diagnosis when the check fails.
# The command still exits non-zero even when --fix is used.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-05-04T12:02:00Z'

cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

git checkout -b feature
gs branch track --base main

# Install a fake 'claude' binary that reads stdin and prints a diagnosis.
mkdir $WORK/bin
cp $WORK/extra/claude $WORK/bin/claude
chmod 755 $WORK/bin/claude
env PATH=$WORK/bin:$PATH

# Write a precommit config whose single check always fails.
mkdir .gitspice
cp $WORK/config/precommit.yaml .gitspice/precommit.yaml
git add .gitspice/precommit.yaml
gs cc -m 'test: add failing precommit check'

# --fix should: still exit non-zero, show boom on stderr,
# and show the CLAUDE-DIAG line on stdout.
! gs run precommit-checks --fix
stderr 'boom'
stdout 'CLAUDE-DIAG'

-- repo/.keep --
-- config/precommit.yaml --
checks:
  - name: boom-check
    cmd: 'echo "boom"; exit 1'
-- extra/claude --
#!/bin/sh
# Fake claude: read stdin (the prompt) and emit a canned diagnosis.
cat
echo 'CLAUDE-DIAG: would suggest a fix'
